title: memory_leak
description: A memory leak in a production application causes gradual service degradation and eventual crashes.

timeline.title: Gradual Memory Growth Detected
timeline.description: Monitoring shows a production application server's memory usage has been growing 2% per hour for 3 days.

timeline.title: Performance Degradation
timeline.description: As memory approaches capacity, the application begins swapping to disk. Response times increase 400%.

timeline.title: Application Crash
timeline.description: The application process is killed by the OOM killer. All requests to this node fail.

timeline.title: Memory Profiling Initiated
timeline.description: Engineers begin profiling a staging replica to identify the source of the memory leak.

timeline.title: Leak Source Identified
timeline.description: Profiling reveals a connection pool that does not release closed connections properly.

timeline.title: Fix Deployed
timeline.description: The connection pool fix is deployed and memory growth stabilizes. Regular restarts scheduled as interim mitigation.

node-1.title: Application OOM Crash
node-1.situation: A production application node has been killed by the OOM killer due to exhausted memory. Monitoring shows memory has been growing steadily for 3 days. The application has been restarted automatically by the process supervisor and is temporarily healthy, but will crash again without intervention.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Implement a temporary scheduled restart every 6 hours as a stop-gap to prevent OOM crashes, while simultaneously initiating memory profiling on a staging replica to identify the leak source.

options.label: B
options.text: Add more RAM to the server to accommodate the growing memory usage without investigating the root cause.

options.label: C
options.text: Simply restart the application and wait to see if the problem recurs before investigating.

node-2.title: Memory Profiling
node-2.situation: Scheduled restarts are keeping production stable. On the staging replica, you have attached a memory profiler. After 2 hours of profiling under simulated production load, the profiler shows heap memory growing specifically in the database connection pool object.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Analyze the connection pool code for resource cleanup issues, focusing on connection close and error handling paths that might leave connections unreleased.

options.label: B
options.text: Disable connection pooling entirely in production as a quick fix without understanding the root cause.

node-3.title: Code Fix Development
node-3.situation: Code review identifies that connection pool cleanup is not called when a database query times out, causing the connection object to remain allocated. A fix has been written that adds proper cleanup in the timeout exception handler. The fix needs validation.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Write an automated test that simulates timeout conditions, verify the fix prevents the leak in staging under extended profiling, then prepare a production deployment with a rollback plan.

options.label: B
options.text: Deploy the fix directly to production without automated testing since the fix looks correct in code review.

node-4.title: Production Deployment
node-4.situation: The fix has passed all automated tests and staging memory profiling shows stable memory over 8 hours. You are ready to deploy the fix to production. You need to plan the rollout strategy.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Deploy the fix using a canary rollout, monitoring memory growth on the canary instance for 2 hours before rolling out to the full fleet, with the scheduled restarts remaining active until the canary confirms stability.

options.label: B
options.text: Deploy the fix to all production instances simultaneously and immediately remove the scheduled restarts.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You resolved the memory leak incident by implementing a temporary mitigation, systematically profiling to find the root cause, developing and testing a fix, and safely deploying it via canary rollout while monitoring for stability.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
